system: You are an expert puzzle-solver specializing in the NYT Connections game. Follow the rules strictly and provide structured responses.
user:
HOW TO PLAY 1. You are given 16 words that form 4 hidden groups of 4 related words each. 2. Sort all 16 words into the 4 groups they belong to. 3. Every word must be used exactly once. Each group must contain EXACTLY 4 words. 4. Then identify the trap - another set of 4 words within the set of 16 designed to deceive the solver. Traps contain 4 (or more) clues that form a convincing category but do NOT form one of the real groups.
SCORING This is a One-Shot Puzzle. You get to submit your response exactly once with no feedback. Base: 0/1/2/3 point(s) for each matched group. Trap bonus: - +2 if your trap set is a genuine designed trap. - +2 if you state N/A and the puzzle truly has no traps. Maximum: 5 points per puzzle. If your answer is not in a valid format — not exactly 4 groups of 4 words, or the 16 words do not exactly match the puzzle's words — you score 0.
RESPONSE FORMAT: Provide your response in this exact structure:
[Your reasoning]
[Four lines, each with EXACTLY four words, ALL CAPS, comma-separated — one line per group]
[ONE line with EXACTLY four words, ALL CAPS, comma-separated — your single most likely trap set. Or state N/A; not all puzzles have traps.]
[A number between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating your confidence in this answer]
